findings.one_line: The feasible-upper proof text closes only by invoking `lem:clipped-score-drift-bound`, `lem:clipped-score-variance-envelope`, `lem:feasible-master-bound`, and `lem:clip-balance-exponent`, but none of those are present in the discharged core or listed in `depends_on`, so this upper-rate derivation is not reproduced from the visible DAG.

The localized variance step is load-bearing for the claimed exponent, but the node’s stated hypotheses do not surface `ass:clipped-propensity-variance-stability`, so the upper bound is conditional on an extra estimator-side assumption rather than proved from the visible assumptions alone.

This theorem advertises a two-sided rate characterization, but its upper half is only imported from the under-supported `oeq:feasible-upper`; on the discharged core, the clean delivered result is the converse plus an incompletely justified feasible bracket.
